vocabulary.chinese: 超出；在……较远的一边；在（或向）更远处
vocabulary.example_sentence: Telescopes let us see worlds beyond our own planet.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 望远镜让我们看见地球以外的世界。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The Mars rover mission searches for signs of ancient water.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 火星探测器任务在寻找古代水的痕迹。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Astronomers aimed the telescope at Proxima Centauri last night.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 天文学家昨晚把望远镜对准比邻星。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The rover rolled slowly across the red dust of Mars.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 探测器缓缓驶过火星的红色尘土。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The first manned mission to the Moon amazed people worldwide.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 首次载人登月任务震惊了全世界。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Engineers maintain the spacecraft so systems stay reliable in orbit.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 工程师维护飞船，让系统在轨道上保持可靠。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Weather delayed the rocket launch by twenty-four hours.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 天气让火箭发射推迟了二十四小时。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Astronauts feel weightless when the craft falls freely around Earth.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 飞行器绕地球自由下落时，宇航员会感到失重。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Even small movement can drift tools away in zero gravity.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 在零重力下，轻微动作也会把工具飘走。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Tired crew members must tie themselves so they do not float asleep.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 疲惫的乘员必须把自己系好，以免睡着时飘走。

vocabulary.example_sentence: It is hard to fall asleep with sunrise every ninety minutes in orbit.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 在轨道上每九十分钟一次日出，很难入睡。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Crew tie sleeping bags to the wall before lights go out.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 熄灯前，乘员把睡袋系在舱壁上。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The rover studies geology to learn how Mars formed.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 探测器研究地质以了解火星如何形成。

vocabulary.example_sentence: A weather satellite sends images of storms back to Earth.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 气象卫星把风暴图像传回地球。

vocabulary.example_sentence: It sounds crazy, but Voyager still sends signals after decades.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 听起来很疯狂，但旅行者号几十年后仍在发信号。

vocabulary.example_sentence: No human being has set foot on Mars yet.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 还没有人类踏上火星。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Long missions require careful planning for food and air.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 长期任务需要仔细规划食物和空气。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The rocket lifted the unmanned probe beyond Earth's atmosphere.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 火箭把无人探测器送出地球大气层。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Officials cancel launches if wind near the pad is too strong.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 发射台附近风太大时，官员会取消发射。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Scientists publish rover data so students can study real Mars rocks.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 科学家公布探测器数据，让学生研究真实的火星岩石。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Each new exoplanet discovery raises questions about life beyond the Sun.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 每发现一颗系外行星，都会引发关于太阳系外生命的疑问。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Solar panels on the station capture energy from the Sun.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 空间站的太阳能板捕获太阳的能量。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Ancient stories named constellations after gods in the sky.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 古代故事用天上的神祇为星座命名。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The dark side of the Moon once seemed mysterious to early astronomers.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 月球背面对早期天文学家曾显得神秘。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The crowded spacecraft carried supplies for a six-month stay.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 拥挤的飞船载着六个月驻留的补给。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Pack extra batteries in case the rover loses power on Mars.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 多带备用电池，以防探测器在火星上断电。

vocabulary.example_sentence: A warning board listed every tool allowed on board the ship.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 警示牌列出了飞船上允许携带的每件工具。

vocabulary.example_sentence: There are six astronauts on board the space station today.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 今天空间站上共有六名宇航员。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Our galaxy holds hundreds of billions of stars.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 我们的星系拥有数千亿颗恒星。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Distances in space are often measured in trillions of metres.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 太空距离常以万亿米计。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Earth looks completely blue when viewed from orbit.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 从轨道上看，地球完全是蓝色的。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Countries cooperate to operate the International Space Station.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 各国合作运营国际空间站。

vocabulary.example_sentence: NASA cooperates with other agencies to survey Mars.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 美国国家航空航天局与其他机构合作探测火星。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Ann dreamed of the day humans would set foot on Mars.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 安梦想着人类踏上火星的那一天。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Voyager is heading toward the edge of our galaxy.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 旅行者号正朝我们星系的边缘飞去。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Unmanned craft reached Mars long before manned flights were planned.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 在计划载人飞行之前，无人飞行器就已到达火星。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The landing craft touched down softly on the dusty plain.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 着陆器轻轻降落在尘土平原上。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The spaceship circled Earth before firing engines toward the Moon.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 飞船绕地球一圈后点火飞向月球。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The orbital survey mapped valleys that might once hold water.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 轨道测绘绘制了可能曾存水的谷地。

vocabulary.example_sentence: The crowded control room cheered when the probe sent its first photo.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 探测器传来第一张照片时，拥挤的控制室欢呼起来。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Specialists operate the telescope from a room far from the dome.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 专家在远离圆顶的房间操作望远镜。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Mars appears red because iron-rich dust covers its surface.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 火星呈红色，因为富含铁的尘土覆盖其表面。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Voyager carries a golden record of sounds from Earth.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 旅行者号携带着一张记录地球声音的金唱片。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Proxima Centauri is the nearest star beyond our solar system.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 比邻星是太阳系外最近的恒星。

vocabulary.example_sentence: Ann wrote a report on how rovers cooperate with satellites in orbit.
vocabulary.example_sentence_zh: 安写了一份关于探测器如何与轨道卫星协作的报告。
